content.problemStatement:
You use the Dotify app to store and play your favorite songs. You have a total of NNN different songs in the app, numbered from 111 to NNN. The ithi^{th}ith song is MiM_iMi​ minutes long, and is in the language LiL_iLi​.

You are going on a long drive, and your friend only knows the language LLL. So you want to create a playlist which satisfies these conditions:


There should be exactly KKK songs in the playlist and they should all be distinct.
Every song in the playlist should be from your list of NNN songs in the app.
Every song in the playlist should be in the language LLL.
The length of a playlist is the sum of the lengths of all the songs in it. Find the maximum length of the playlist that you can create while satisfying the conditions above. If such a playlist cannot be created, output -1.


Input Format

The first line of input will contain a single integer TTT, denoting the number of test cases.
Each test case consists of multiple lines of input.

The first line of each test case contains three space-separated integers NNN, KKK, and LLL — the total number of songs in the app, the number of songs needed in the playlist, and the language of the songs in the playlist, respectively.
The next NNN lines describe the songs. The iii-th of these NNN lines contains two space-separated integers MiM_iMi​ and LiL_iLi​, denoting the length and language of the ithi^{th}ith song in the app.




Output Format
For each test case, output on a new line the length of the longest playlist that you can create.
content.inputFormat: The first line contains an integer T, the number of test cases. For each test case: the first line contains three integers N, K and L. Then N lines follow, each containing two integers M_i and L_i — the length and language of the i-th song.
content.outputFormat: For each test case output a single line containing the maximum possible total length of a playlist that consists of exactly K distinct songs all in language L. If it is impossible, output -1.

sampleTestCases.explanation:
Test case 1: only songs with language 2 are lengths 5 and 7, pick the longest (7).
Test case 2: need 2 songs of language 2, both available (5 and 7) sum to 12.
Test case 3: no song of language 1, impossible => -1.
Test case 4: one song of language 4 with length 8, answer 8.
Songs with language 3 have lengths 4,9,5. The three longest are all three, sum = 4+9+5 = 18.
Language 5 songs: 10,3,2. Pick the two largest: 10+3 = 13.

solution.approach: For each test case we collect the lengths of all songs whose language equals the required language L. If the number of such songs is less than K, the answer is -1. Otherwise we sort the collected lengths in descending order and sum the first K values – this gives the maximum possible total length because picking the K longest eligible songs yields the largest sum.
solution.timeComplexity: O(N log N) per test case due to sorting the eligible songs.
solution.spaceComplexity: O(N) for storing the eligible lengths.
